preview.summary: Two sides chasing a Group B knockout berth meet in Seattle, with playoff-hardened Bosnia and Herzegovina facing Asian champions Qatar. Expect Bosnia to lean on Edin Dzeko's experience while Qatar build through their creative wide play.
preview.homeForm: Bosnia reached the finals via the playoffs, beating Wales and Italy on penalties after finishing second in their qualifying group.
preview.awayForm: Qatar, ranked 55th, qualified on the pitch for the first time, riding the Asian qualifying goals of Almoez Ali.
preview.homeKey: Edin Dzeko, 40-year-old all-time top scorer still finding the net.
preview.awayKey: Akram Afif, two-time Asian Player of the Year and chief creator.
storylines: Dzeko's late-career World Cup bow after dramatic playoffs
Qatar's first qualified World Cup appearance after 2022 host entry
Both sides eyeing a maiden run to the round of 16
preview.h2h: The teams have met only once, a draw, with no prior competitive history between them.
